cards.title: The problem in one screen
cards.notes: Open on the pain, not the product. Show the messy before: polling a REST endpoint on a cron, missing events, stale data. Ten seconds, tops — then promise the fix.

snippets.label: Old polling snippet
snippets.content: while true; do curl -s {{apiBase}}/events | jq '.[]'; sleep 30; done

cards.title: Grab an API key
cards.notes: Keys are self-serve — say so. Copy the demo key onto the clipboard so every subsequent request just works. Mention scopes exist without going down the rabbit hole.

cards.title: First request
cards.notes: The hello-world moment. Run one curl, get real JSON back. This proves the API is up and the key works before anything fancy.

cards.title: Use the SDK
cards.notes: Now show the ergonomic path. Same call, three lines of typed code. Contrast with the raw curl to make the DX win land.

snippets.label: SDK usage (TS)

cards.title: Subscribe to a webhook
cards.notes: This is the headline: stop polling, get pushed. Register the webhook live and show the endpoint receive a real {{eventType}} event. Have the payload ready so you can talk through the shape.

cards.title: Recap & docs
cards.notes: Tie it together: key → request → SDK → webhook. Point at the docs and the quickstart repo. Invite questions. Don't add new material here — just land the plane.
